publicationStmt.publisher: Die Schriftkultur des christlichen Äthiopiens und Eritreas: Eine multimediale Forschungsumgebung / Beta maṣāḥǝft

availability.licence: This file is licensed under the Creative Commons Attribution-ShareAlike 4.0.

msItem.title: Dǝrsān za-darasa Yoḥannǝs ʾAfa Warq ... baʾǝnta kǝbru la-Yoḥannǝs maṭmǝq
On (the Life and Decollation of) John the Baptist
Martyrium of Mammes, Theodotus and his wife Rufina
Gadl wa-sǝmʿ za-qǝddus liqa ḥarā Fāsiladas
Martyrium of Eustathius, his wife and his two children
Gadl wa-sǝmʿ za-qǝddus Qoṗrǝyānos wa-qǝddǝst ʾIyusṭā
Hagiographic Dossier of St George of Lydda
Miracle of George: Andrew and his wife build a church for the body of the saint
Miracle of St George: St George frees a possessed man from Satan
Miracle of St George: St George punishes the thieving Jewish magician
Miracle of St George: St George heals Nāṭolis from leprosy, son of the Persian governor Niqoros
Miracle of St George: Two Samaritans are rescued from being eaten by lions
Miracle of St George: St George heals Socrates from the gout and his possessed son
Miracle of St George: St George punishes a man who stole church’s property
Miracle of St George: St George punishes to death governor ʾAwhǝyos after his attempt to destroy the saint’s church in Joppe
Miracle of St George: The impoverished ship owner Eulogius recovers his money from the Egyptian thief
Miracle of St George: St George punishes a man who refused to pay back the money to a priest
Miracle of St George: A widow offers two pillars to St George’s church in Lydda
Miracle of St George: George, son of Bifān, is rescued from Arab captivity
Miracle of St George: A Jewish woman who comes to the church of St George is healed and protected from her relatives
Zenāhomu la-sabʾa Nāgrān wa-sǝmʿomu la-qǝddus Ḫirut wa-ʾǝlla mǝslehu
Gadl wa-sǝmʿ za-Ṗeṭros liqa ṗāṗṗāsāt za-ʾƎlla ʾƎskǝndǝryā
Zenā la-qǝddǝst ʾAwgānyā wa-zenā ʾabu-hā qǝddus Filǝṗṗos
Dǝrsān za-ʾabbā Sāmuʾel mastagādl za-Dabra Qalamon
Vita and Martyrdom of ʾAbbā Baʾamin
Gadlu la-qǝddus samāʿt wa-ḫayyāl wa-mastagādǝl ʾAnqiṭos wa-ʾǝlla mǝslehu
Gadl wa-sǝmʿ za-qǝddus Marmǝhnām walda Sanāḥereb nǝguśa ʾAtor wa-za-ʾǝḫǝtu Sārā
Gadl wa-sǝmʿ za-qǝddus Gorgoryos liqa ṗāṗṗāsāt za-ʾArmānyā
Combat of ‘abbā Absādi and bishop ‘Alāniqos from Egypt
Gadl wa-sǝmʿ za-qǝddus Tewodros Banādlewos za-hagara ʾAnṣokiyā
Zenāhomu la-7daqiq za-kona samāʿt ba-wǝsta baʾat
Gadl wa-sǝmʿ za-qǝddus wa-kǝbur samāʿt nǝṣuh ʾabbā Pifāmon
Gadl wa-sǝmʿ za-qǝddǝst Ṭeqalā wa-za-4-danāgǝl ʾǝlla mǝsle-hā
Sǝmʿ za-qǝddusān ʾAbuqir wa-Yoḥannǝs ʾǝḫuhu wa-śalās danāgǝl wa-ʾǝmmon

supportDesc.foliation: Foliation by the library in the upper right corner.

collation.signatures: Quire marks in the upper inner corner of the first folium of each quire until . Numeration starts anew with . The quire marks of and are decorated.

layout.ab: Primary pricks and horizontal pricks are visible.
layout.note: Vertical ruled lines mostly stop at the primary pricks, but also go beyond them.
layout.ab: The upper line is written above the ruling, the lower line is written above the ruling.
Primary pricks and horizontal pricks are visible.
layout.note: Vertical ruled lines mostly stop at the primary pricks, but also go beyond them.
layout.ab: The upper line is written above the ruling, the lower line is written above the ruling.
Primary pricks and horizontal pricks are visible.
layout.note: Vertical ruled lines mostly stop at the primary pricks, but also go beyond them.
layout.ab: The upper line is written above the ruling, the lower line is written above the ruling.

handNote.desc: Written in good hand. ʾEṗifānyos and Yonās are mentioned as scribes in a supplication on , but no change of hand could be clearly observed.

decoDesc.summary: Numerous miniatures in the , mostly extending over part of a leaf, but occasionally covering the entire leaf.

item.desc: The name of the commissioner, mentioned in supplications throughout, was Walda Giyorgis ().
The dates on which the texts are to be read are written in the upper margin of many of the texts.
The note states that the manuscript belongs to the church of Madḫāne ʿĀlam.

binding.decoNote: Two wooden boards. Both inner boards are darkened around the edges, possibly showing traces of a former cover.
Blind-tooled leather quarter cover (with the gilded title and shelfmark on the spine).
Sewing stations are used in an irregular way.
The manuscript has been rebound in the library. Possibly both wooden boards have been replaced, and a blind-tooled quarter cover added.

history.provenance: The manuscript had very probably belonged to a church in the Gondar area, from where it was taken by Tewodros and brought to Madḫāne ʿĀlam. It was then looted by the British Napier expedition in 1868.

bibl.title: Catalogue of the Ethiopic Manuscripts in the British Museum Acquired since the Year 1847

projectDesc.p: Encoded according to TEI P5 Guidelines.

encodingDesc.p: Encoded according to the Beta maṣāḥǝft Guidelines. These Guidelines detail the TEI format ruled by the Beta maṣāḥǝft Schema. The present TEI file is enriched with an Xquery transformation taking advantage of the exist-db database instance where the data is stored and of the many external resources to which this data points to.

calendar.p: ʿĀmata ʿālam/ʿĀmata ʾəm-fəṭrat (Era of the World)
ʿĀmata śəggāwe (Era of the Incarnation – Ethiopian)

t:revisionDesc.change: Continued description from historical catalogue entry with consultation of images
